38.reason: [if input ends with '=', this sets b[j-1] to '\0', but the '=' is treated as an operator, which breaks logic; also, if expression has no operators (e.g., "5="), j becomes 1, then b[0]='\0', but later code assumes b contains only +,-,*,/]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.2 , reason:  [if input ends with '=', this sets b[j-1] to '\0', but the '=' is treated as an operator, which breaks logic; also, if expression has no operators (e.g., "5="), j becomes 1, then b[0]='\0', but later code assumes b contains only +,-,*,/]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.2 , reason:  [This assumes the last character is '=', so b[j-1] removes it. But if the expression ends with '=', then the last non-digit is '=', which is stored in b[j], then j is incremented. So b[j-1] is '=', and setting b[j-1]='\0' removes it. This part may work, but the parsing logic fails when numbers have multiple digits because d[j] is reused without resetting for new numbers. Specifically, after an operator, d[j] should start fresh, but it continues from previous value.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.2 , reason:  [if input ends with '=', then b[j-1] is '=', but later code treats b as only containing operators +,-,*,/; also, when expression has no operators (e.g., "5="), j becomes 1, so b[0]='=', then b[j-1]='\0' sets b[0]='\0', which breaks logic]

99.reason: [This sets `flag` to the next number, but what if there are consecutive * or / later? The logic tries to handle operator precedence by looking ahead, but it's flawed. Also, `ans` is set to remember sign, but this approach doesn't correctly accumulate chained multiplications/divisions.]
